Description: Verifies the entire nightly synchronization process triggers and runs successfully end-to-end.
Pre-conditions: Scheduler is active; Rainbow API is online; Salesforce API is online
Test Steps: 1. Nightly cron trigger fires | 2. Deletes stale records in Salesforce | 3. Fetches registrations from Rainbow | 4. Fetches owners and business partners | 5. Maps to flat landowners | 6. Upserts to Salesforce | 7. Logs sync completion summary
Expected Result: All current landowners are synchronized to Salesforce LandOwner__c and stale records are purged.

Description: Verifies that landowner records that have expired (ToDate__c is in the past) and have no manually assigned lawyer are deleted.
Pre-conditions: Salesforce contains LandOwner__c records with ToDate__c < TODAY and Lawyer__c = null
Test Steps: 1. Query Salesforce for expired records without lawyer | 2. Stale records are returned | 3. Call Salesforce Delete on stale IDs | 4. Log count of deleted records
Expected Result: Stale records are successfully deleted from Salesforce and count logged.

Description: Verifies that expired landowner records that have a manually assigned lawyer are preserved.
Pre-conditions: Salesforce contains LandOwner__c records with ToDate__c < TODAY and Lawyer__c != null
Test Steps: 1. Query Salesforce for expired records without lawyer | 2. Expired records with lawyer are excluded from results | 3. Salesforce Delete is not called for these IDs
Expected Result: Records with a Lawyer__c lookup filled are not deleted from Salesforce.

Description: Verifies that the purge sub-flow completes gracefully without invoking delete if no stale records exist.
Pre-conditions: Salesforce contains no LandOwner__c records with ToDate__c < TODAY and Lawyer__c = null
Test Steps: 1. Query Salesforce for expired records without lawyer | 2. Query returns empty list | 3. Skip Delete invocation | 4. Set deleteCount to 0 and log
Expected Result: The sub-flow skips delete invocation and logs 0 deleted records.

Description: Verifies that registrations are successfully fetched from Rainbow when all records fit on a single page.
Pre-conditions: Rainbow OData API is active and returns one page of registrations
Test Steps: 1. Send GET to /registrations with filter | 2. Receive HTTP 200 payload | 3. Map value array to registrations list | 4. Verify properties are mapped correctly
Expected Result: A single page of registrations is successfully fetched and mapped.

Description: Verifies that registrations are successfully fetched and merged when multiple pages exist via nextLink.
Pre-conditions: Rainbow OData API returns a nextLink page header
Test Steps: 1. Send GET to /registrations | 2. Receive page with @odata.nextLink | 3. Request next path page | 4. Repeat until nextLink is null | 5. Concat and map all values
Expected Result: All pages are traversed and merged into a single registrations array.

Description: Verifies that Rainbow API connection timeouts/connectivity failures trigger retries before failing.
Test Steps: 1. Send GET to /registrations | 2. Request fails with timeout | 3. Wait 2 seconds | 4. Retry request | 5. Succeed on retry | 6. Process output
Expected Result: The HTTP client retries and successfully retrieves registrations after transient error.

Description: Verifies that owners are successfully fetched for a given property registration.
Pre-conditions: Rainbow OData API is active; vars.reg contains a valid registrationKey
Test Steps: 1. Send GET to /owners with registrationKey filter | 2. Receive HTTP 200 payload | 3. Store owners array in variable
Expected Result: Owners list is retrieved and saved in variable.

Description: Verifies that if fetching owners fails with a network error the system logs the failure and continues without crashing.
Pre-conditions: Rainbow API returns a timeout error
Test Steps: 1. Send GET to /owners | 2. Request fails | 3. Swallowed by On Error Continue | 4. Log warning | 5. Set vars.owners to empty array | 6. Continue loop
Expected Result: The failure is logged and the sync process continues to next registration.

Description: Verifies that business partner details are successfully fetched for an owner.
Test Steps: 1. Send GET to /businessPartners with partner ID filter | 2. Receive BP payload | 3. Save details in businessPartners map

Description: Verifies that if fetching a business partner fails
Pre-conditions: the landowner is still mapped using whatever details exist.
Input Data: 1. Send GET to /businessPartners | 2. Request fails | 3. On Error Continue catches error | 4. Add empty object to BP map | 5. Continue processing owner
Expected Status: The failure is logged and landowner is built using available owner fields.

Description: Verifies mapping registration + owner + business partner to a flat record with a Building Lookup relationship.
Test Steps: 1. Execute build-landowner DataWeave | 2. Execute add-building-lookup DataWeave | 3. Verify Building__r lookup is added
Expected Result: Output record has Project__r and Building__r relationship objects attached.

Description: Verifies mapping registration + owner + business partner when buildingCode is null is project-level only.
Test Steps: 1. Execute build-landowner DataWeave | 2. Execute add-building-lookup DataWeave | 3. Verify BuildingCode__c key is stripped
Expected Result: Output record has Project__r but no Building__r or BuildingCode__c fields.

Description: Verifies bulk upserting flat landowner records to Salesforce in chunks of 200.
Pre-conditions: landownerBatch contains 202 flat records; Salesforce API is active
Test Steps: 1. Chunk batch into two parts (200 + 2) | 2. Call upsert on LandOwner__c using External_Key__c | 3. Success returned for all records | 4. Set upsertCount to 202
Expected Result: All records are successfully upserted to Salesforce.

Description: Verifies that individual record failures are logged and do not abort the entire batch upsert.
Pre-conditions: landownerBatch contains records; Salesforce returns partial success (one record fails validation)
Test Steps: 1. Call upsert on LandOwner__c | 2. Salesforce returns success=true for first record, success=false for second | 3. Log external ID of failing record | 4. Increment failedCount
Expected Result: Successful records are saved and failures are logged with external keys.

Description: Verifies that Salesforce connection failures abort the scheduler run completely.
Test Steps: 1. Call upsert on LandOwner__c | 2. Connection timeout error thrown | 3. Caught by global-error-handler | 4. Log FATAL message | 5. Abort run
Expected Result: The run is terminated and a critical error is logged for admin alerts.

Description: Verifies that the HTTP GET /health endpoint returns 200 OK for operational status monitoring.
Test Steps: 1. Send HTTP GET request to /health | 2. Receive HTTP response
Expected Result: Response returns HTTP 200 status code with "OK" body.
